questions.q: A monochromatic light of wavelength 6000 Å falls on a soap film of thickness 0.002 mm. The minimum thickness of the film that will make it invisible is?
questions.explanation: The soap film will be invisible when the light reflected from the two surfaces is in phase. This occurs when the path difference is an integral multiple of the wavelength.

questions.q: A lens forms a real and inverted image of a candle flame on a screen 120 cm from the lens. If the candle flame is moved 5 cm towards the lens, the image will be?
questions.explanation: When the object is moved towards the lens, the image will shift towards the lens. Since the image is real and inverted, it will become virtual and upright.

questions.q: The focal length of a convex lens is 15 cm. The power of the lens is?
questions.explanation: Power of a lens is given by P = 1/f, where f is the focal length in meters. Therefore, P = 1/0.15 = 6.67 D, which is approximately equal to 6 D.

questions.q: A parallel beam of light falls on a convex lens of focal length 20 cm. The nature of the image formed is?
questions.explanation: When a parallel beam of light falls on a convex lens, the image is real and inverted.

questions.q: The refractive index of a prism is 1.5. The angle of deviation for a ray of light passing through the prism is?
questions.explanation: The angle of deviation is given by δ = (μ - 1)A, where μ is the refractive index and A is the angle of the prism. Therefore, δ = (1.5 - 1)A = 0.5A.

questions.q: A ray of light is incident on a plane mirror at an angle of incidence of 30°. The angle of reflection is?
questions.explanation: The angle of reflection is equal to the angle of incidence.

questions.q: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm is placed in contact with a concave lens of focal length -30 cm. The focal length of the combination is?
questions.explanation: The focal length of the combination is given by 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2, where f1 and f2 are the focal lengths of the individual lenses.

questions.q: A parallel beam of light falls on a convex lens of focal length 20 cm. The image formed is 40 cm from the lens. The object distance is?
questions.explanation: The image distance is given by 1/v = 1/f - 1/u, where v is the image distance, f is the focal length, and u is the object distance.

questions.q: A ray of light passes through a prism of angle 30° and is deviated by 10°. The refractive index of the prism is?
questions.explanation: The angle of deviation is given by δ = (μ - 1)A, where μ is the refractive index and A is the angle of the prism.

questions.q: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm is placed in contact with a plane mirror. The image formed by the combination is?
questions.explanation: The image formed by the combination is virtual and upright.

questions.q: A concave lens of focal length -30 cm is placed in contact with a convex lens of focal length 20 cm. The focal length of the combination is?
questions.explanation: The focal length of the combination is given by 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2, where f1 and f2 are the focal lengths of the individual lenses.

questions.q: A ray of light passes through a prism of angle 30° and is deviated by 5°. The refractive index of the prism is?
questions.explanation: The angle of deviation is given by δ = (μ - 1)A, where μ is the refractive index and A is the angle of the prism.

questions.q: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm is placed in contact with a concave lens of focal length -30 cm. The nature of the image formed is?
questions.explanation: The image formed by the combination is diverging.

questions.q: A ray of light is incident on a plane mirror at an angle of incidence of 60°. The angle of reflection is?
questions.explanation: The angle of reflection is equal to the angle of incidence.

questions.q: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm is placed in contact with a plane mirror. The image formed by the combination is 40 cm from the lens. The object distance is?
questions.explanation: The image distance is given by 1/v = 1/f - 1/u, where v is the image distance, f is the focal length, and u is the object distance.

questions.q: A ray of light passes through a prism of angle 30° and is deviated by 15°. The refractive index of the prism is?
questions.explanation: The angle of deviation is given by δ = (μ - 1)A, where μ is the refractive index and A is the angle of the prism.

questions.q: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm is placed in contact with a concave lens of focal length -30 cm. The focal length of the combination is 10 cm. The focal length of the individual lenses is?
questions.explanation: The focal length of the combination is given by 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2, where f1 and f2 are the focal lengths of the individual lenses.

questions.q: A ray of light passes through a prism of angle 30° and is deviated by 20°. The refractive index of the prism is?
questions.explanation: The angle of deviation is given by δ = (μ - 1)A, where μ is the refractive index and A is the angle of the prism.

questions.q: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm is placed in contact with a plane mirror. The image formed by the combination is virtual and upright. The object distance is?
questions.explanation: The image formed by the combination is virtual and upright.

questions.q: A concave lens of focal length -30 cm is placed in contact with a convex lens of focal length 20 cm. The focal length of the combination is -10 cm. The focal length of the individual lenses is?
questions.explanation: The focal length of the combination is given by 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2, where f1 and f2 are the focal lengths of the individual lenses.
